01 — Logo

Your mark, cleaned up.

The full wordmark, the standalone icon (spark), and approved background versions. Use the full wordmark wherever there is room; let the icon stand on its own for avatars, favicons, and tight placements. Hover any logo to download it.

Wordmark — Black · on light
Wordmark — White · on dark
Icon — Black · avatar / favicon
Icon — White · avatar / favicon
Tagline lockup — “One Day At A Time” · apparel & embroidery

02 — Color

Four colors.
One clear voice.

A high-contrast, confident palette. Ink black and pure white do the heavy lifting; deep brown is a warm accent and warm sand is a soft supporting field. Click any swatch to copy its HEX.

Pure White#FFFFFF
Deep Brown#4E312D
Warm Sand#C7B299

03 — Typography

One typeface.
Two voices.

Invisible Challenge is set in Acumin Pro ExtraCondensed. The bold italic carries headlines and brand moments; the extra-light weight handles secondary lines and quieter statements. Both real weights are embedded in this guide.

Display / Headlines
Invisible Challenge
ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ
abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz 0123456789
Acumin Pro ExtraCondensed — Semibold Italic
Licensed Adobe typeface · embedded for display
Use for: the wordmark, headlines, signage, campaign lines, major brand statements.
Secondary / Supporting
One day at a time
ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ
abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz 0123456789
Acumin Pro ExtraCondensed — Extra Light
Licensed Adobe typeface · embedded for display
Use for: descriptors, taglines, eyebrows, labels, and quieter supporting text.
Body text: Acumin ExtraCondensed is a display face, so long paragraphs and UI copy are set in a clean neutral sans (as shown in this guide) to keep small text readable. Reserve Acumin for brand moments, not body blocks.

05 — Usage

A few rules
worth keeping.

Simple rules to keep the Invisible Challenge mark sharp and consistent everywhere it shows up.

Do this
  • Use approved logo versions only
  • Keep clear space around the mark
  • Reverse to white on dark backgrounds
  • Use the icon for avatars and favicons
  • Pair the bold italic with extra-light
  • Use brown and sand as accents
Not this
  • Stretch, rotate, or distort the mark
  • Place the black logo on dark fields
  • Recolor outside the approved palette
  • Add shadows, gradients, or effects
  • Crowd the logo with other elements
  • Rebuild or redraw the icon
